text: This recommendation provides a formal definition of the "halogen bond," a term used to categorize a specific class of noncovalent interactions where halogen atoms serve as electrophilic sites. A halogen bond is defined by a net attractive interaction between an electron-poor region of a halogen atom within a molecular entity and a nucleophilic region of another, or the same, molecular entity. To assist in identifying this interaction, the document outlines the general architectural features of the R–X···Y system—comprising a halogen bond donor (R–X) and an acceptor (Y)—and lists common chemical examples of each. Additionally, a set of experimental and theoretical criteria is provided to characterize the bond, including geometric parameters such as high directionality and sub-van der Waals interatomic distances, as well as spectroscopic signatures in NMR, IR, and UV-vis formats. These guidelines aim to standardize the nomenclature and identification of halogen bonding within the broader context of supramolecular chemistry and crystal engineering.
